content.summary: Setting feedback decay/multiply values above 1.0 causes exponential brightness growth, eventually blowing out to white. This is a common pitfall when tuning feedback loops.
warnings: Always clamp decay uniforms to 0.0-1.0 range or use a Level TOP with post-multiply

payload.cause: A decay multiplier > 1.0 amplifies the feedback signal each frame, causing exponential growth that saturates to white within seconds.
payload.fix: Clamp the decay uniform to [0.0, 1.0] range. In GLSL: clamp(uDecay, 0.0, 1.0). In the parameter page: set Max to 1.0 with Clamp enabled.
payload.symptom: Feedback loop output gradually or instantly turns solid white/bright. Image blows out and never recovers.
